channel.title: AI News — A Guide to Cloud & AI
channel.description: Daily AI news digest — curated headlines, summaries and trends

item.title: On-Device Function Calling in Google AI Edge Gallery
item.description: Google has launched FunctionGemma, a 270M parameter model that enables efficient on-device function calling for mobile AI applications, allowing tasks to be performed offline with low latency. This development enhances user experience by enabling complex functions like calendar management and game logic execution directly on devices.

item.title: Supercharge your AI agents: The New ADK Integrations Ecosystem
item.description: The Agent Development Kit (ADK) now integrates with various third-party tools, allowing developers to connect their AI agents to platforms like GitHub and Notion, facilitating the creation of real-world applications. This ecosystem aims to enhance the functionality and versatility of AI agents.

item.title: How we built the Google I/O 2026 Save the Date experience
item.description: Google I/O 2026 is scheduled for May 19-20, featuring a save the date puzzle that showcases how AI can enhance event experiences. This annual tradition builds excitement for the event while highlighting AI's role in innovation.

item.title: You can't stream the energy: A developer's guide to Google Cloud Next '26 in Vegas
item.description: Google Cloud Next '26 in Las Vegas will focus on in-person networking and hands-on problem-solving, featuring sessions on agentic AI and Gemini multimodal breakthroughs. The event emphasizes the importance of direct interaction for developers in navigating complex tech landscapes.

item.title: What's new in TensorFlow 2.21
item.description: Google has launched LiteRT, the successor to TFLite, which offers improved GPU and NPU acceleration and supports PyTorch and JAX. This update enhances TensorFlow's efficiency and security, positioning LiteRT as the primary framework for deploying advanced AI applications.

item.title: Introducing Wednesday Build Hour
item.description: Wednesday Build Hour is a new weekly session by Google Cloud aimed at helping developers enhance their cloud skills through hands-on activities. This initiative focuses on practical applications of AI and developer tools, providing immediate benefits to participants.

item.title: Unleash Your Development Superpowers: Refining the Core Coding Experience
item.description: The Gemini Code Assist team has rolled out updates to improve coding workflows with tools like Agent Mode and Inline Diff Views. These enhancements aim to make AI a more effective collaborator tailored to individual developer needs.

item.title: OpenAI acquires TBPN, the buzzy founder-led business talk show
item.description: OpenAI has acquired TBPN, a popular tech podcast, which will continue to operate independently under the oversight of Chris Lehane. This acquisition reflects OpenAI's interest in expanding its media presence and influence in the tech community.

item.title: PSA: Anyone with a link can view your Granola notes by default
item.description: Users of the AI note-taking app Granola should review their privacy settings, as notes are viewable by anyone with a link by default, and are used for internal AI training unless opted out. This raises concerns about user privacy and data security.

item.title: New Rowhammer attacks give complete control of machines running Nvidia GPUs
item.description: New Rowhammer attacks, GDDRHammer and GeForge, exploit vulnerabilities in Nvidia GPUs, allowing attackers to gain complete control over affected machines. This development poses significant security risks for systems utilizing these GPUs.

item.title: Microsoft takes on AI rivals with three new foundational models
item.description: Microsoft has launched three new foundational AI models capable of transcribing voice to text and generating audio and images, enhancing its competitive edge in AI. This move follows the formation of the MAI group six months ago, aiming to challenge rivals in the AI space.

item.title: Google now lets you direct avatars through prompts in its Vids app
item.description: Google's Vids app now allows users to customize and direct avatars through prompts, enhancing user interaction in video creation. This feature aims to make content creation more engaging and user-friendly.

item.title: Elon Musk is about to be a very busy boy!
item.description: Elon Musk is expected to file for SpaceX's IPO on June 7th, generating significant anticipation in the tech and investment communities. This potential move reflects Musk's ongoing influence in the aerospace sector.

item.title: OpenAI just bought TBPN
item.description: OpenAI has acquired TBPN, an online talk show that features interviews with AI and tech leaders. This acquisition aims to enhance OpenAI's engagement with the tech community and expand its media presence.

item.title: It’s not easy to get depression-detecting AI through the FDA
item.description: Kintsugi, a startup focused on AI for detecting depression and anxiety, is shutting down after failing to secure FDA clearance. The company plans to release most of its technology as open-source, potentially benefiting future developments.

item.title: Microsoft’s new ‘superintelligence’ game plan is all about business
item.description: Microsoft's Mustafa Suleyman is focusing on developing 'superintelligence' as part of a restructuring effort. This strategic shift aims to position Microsoft as a leader in advanced AI solutions.

item.title: Google Home’s latest update makes Gemini better at understanding your commands
item.description: Google Home's latest update enhances the Gemini AI assistant's ability to understand user commands, making smart home control more intuitive. Users can now describe lighting preferences in more creative ways.

item.title: Welcome Gemma 4: Frontier multimodal intelligence on device
item.description: Gemma 4 has been introduced as a frontier multimodal intelligence device, although specific details about its capabilities were not provided. This launch signifies advancements in on-device AI technology.

item.title: Training an AI Chatbot to Manage Health in Underserved Populations: Methodological Approach
item.description: Research is underway to train an AI chatbot to manage health in underserved populations, addressing significant health disparities. This approach aims to improve healthcare access for vulnerable groups.

item.title: Obfuscation is not security – AI can deobfuscate any minified JavaScript code
item.description: The discussion around AI's ability to deobfuscate minified JavaScript code highlights concerns over security and source code leaks. This situation emphasizes the need for better security measures in software development.

item.title: The Survivalist’s Odds ‘n Sods
item.description: SurvivalBlog's latest column features various news relevant to survivalists and preppers, aiming to educate readers on emergency preparedness.

item.title: SharePoint: Extending AI in SharePoint using custom skills
item.description: SharePoint introduces custom skills for its AI Agent, allowing businesses to create tailored solutions that enhance functionality and workflows.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Improved organization for muted and meeting chats
item.description: Microsoft Teams enhances chat organization by grouping muted and meeting chats into dedicated sections, improving user experience.

item.title: Microsoft Viva: Following feed in Viva Engage
item.description: Microsoft Viva Engage introduces a new feed type to help users stay updated on posts from followed individuals and communities.

item.title: Microsoft Purview: Data Catalog – Migration tool in Unified Catalog to migrate classic glossary terms to Unified Catalog
item.description: Microsoft Purview's Unified Catalog now includes a migration tool to transition classic glossary terms to the new system.

item.title: Microsoft Teams: Facilitator detects and answers questions
item.description: Teams' Facilitator feature now detects unanswered questions during meetings and offers to find answers using web searches.

item.title: django-traduire 0.1.0
item.description: django-traduire 0.1.0 allows automatic translation of django-modeltranslation fields using popular translation services.

item.title: django-traduire added to PyPI
item.description: django-traduire has been added to PyPI, enabling automatic translation for Django model fields using various translation tools.

item.title: Blind artist to run marathon using AI glasses and app in ‘world first’
item.description: Blind artist Clarke Reynolds plans to run the Brighton Marathon with assistance from AI glasses and an app, marking a world first.

item.title: Bitcoin Exchange Inflows Flash Rare Signal As Large Deposits Return
item.description: Recent data indicates a surge in Bitcoin exchange inflows, suggesting that large investors, or 'whales', are re-entering the market.

item.title: "AIの棚取り"できていますか？--ChatGPTやGeminiでのブランド推薦状況を可視化するB2B向け診断ツール「AIタナドリ」を無料公開
item.description: Orosy Corporation has launched 'AIタナドリ', a free diagnostic tool for B2B that visualizes brand recommendations using ChatGPT and Gemini. This tool aims to enhance businesses' understanding of AI's impact on branding.

item.title: 10 Great Sci-Fi Movies You Can Now Stream For Free
item.description: Several streaming platforms are now offering free access to a selection of underrated and classic sci-fi movies, catering to fans looking for quality content to binge-watch. This initiative highlights the ongoing popularity of the sci-fi genre.

item.title: nteract 2.1.1a202604012243
item.description: Interact 2.1.1a202604012243 introduces AI capabilities to Jupyter notebooks, enabling integration with various AI models like Claude and ChatGPT. This update aims to enhance the functionality of data science workflows.

item.title: The SpaceX IPO is great — but it won’t deliver 100x returns
item.description: The article discusses the changing landscape of IPOs in the U.S., noting that companies now face higher thresholds for going public. This shift impacts investors' expectations regarding returns.

item.title: mscs 2.2.0
item.description: The release of mscs 2.2.0 offers a secure and fast serialization method for Python, serving as a replacement for the traditional pickle method. It supports numpy arrays and PyTorch tensors, enhancing data handling.

item.title: mscs added to PyPI
item.description: The mscs library has been added to PyPI, providing a secure serialization option for Python. It is designed to work seamlessly with numpy arrays and PyTorch tensors.

item.title: SpaceX Files Draft for Potentially Stratospheric IPO
item.description: SpaceX has filed a draft for a potential IPO that could raise $75 billion, although challenges remain in the market. This move indicates SpaceX's ambition for significant growth.

item.title: claude-jacked 0.32.0
item.description: The release of claude-jacked 0.32.0 introduces smart reviewers and session search capabilities for Claude Code, enhancing user interaction and functionality.

item.title: language-model-common 1.0.28
item.description: The language-model-common 1.0.28 framework has been released to improve langchain and add loading configurations, facilitating better integration of language models.

item.title: Sona Raises $45 Million to Expand AI Platform for Frontline Businesses
item.description: Sona has successfully raised $45 million in a Series B funding round to expand its AI platform aimed at frontline businesses in sectors like hospitality and healthcare. This funding will help enhance its service offerings.

item.title: pitloom 0.3.0
item.description: Pitloom 0.3.0 introduces automated transparency for Python and AI projects, enabling SBOM generation and metadata extraction from various model formats.

item.title: uipath-langchain 0.9.15
item.description: The uipath-langchain 0.9.15 SDK allows developers to create and deploy LangGraph agents on the UiPath Cloud Platform, expanding automation capabilities.

item.title: Intel Next-Gen Nova Lake-HX Laptop CPUs Pack Up To 28 Cores, AMD Halo Competitor Arrives As Razer Lake-AX
item.description: Intel's upcoming Nova Lake-HX laptop CPUs will feature up to 28 cores, marking a significant upgrade aimed at competing with AMD's offerings.

item.title: dobby-ai-sdk 0.1.0
item.description: The Dobby AI SDK 0.1.0 is officially launched, providing a platform for developers to create AI agents.

item.title: dobby-ai-sdk added to PyPI
item.description: The Dobby AI SDK has been added to PyPI, making it accessible for developers to build AI agents.

item.title: torchio 1.0.2
item.description: Torchio 1.0.2 offers tools for medical image processing using PyTorch, enhancing capabilities in healthcare applications.

item.title: azure-ai-evaluation 1.16.3
item.description: Microsoft's Azure AI Evaluation Library for Python has been updated to version 1.16.3, providing enhanced tools for AI evaluation.

item.title: Project Hail Mary & Artemis II Prove 2026 Is The Year Space Came Back
item.description: The successful launches of Artemis II and Project Hail Mary signal a resurgence in space exploration, with 2026 poised for significant advancements.

item.title: knowledge2 added to PyPI
item.description: Knowledge2, a new Python SDK for the Knowledge2 retrieval platform, has been added to PyPI, enhancing data retrieval capabilities.

item.title: 5 Honest Lessons on How AI is Actually Rewiring B2B Sales (From Salesforce, Momentum, and Mangomint)
item.description: Industry leaders shared insights at SaaStr AI London on how AI is transforming B2B sales, highlighting practical lessons learned.
